rule.name: Freertos Unsafe String Copy API
rule.rule_desc: Detects unsafe string APIs common in memory-corruption vulnerabilities.
rule.vuln_desc: Unsafe string handling may lead to buffer overflows and memory corruption.
rule.developer: Use bounded alternatives (snprintf/strlcpy) and explicit length checks.
rule.reviewer: Review bounds checks around unsafe C string APIs. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Freertos Command Execution Sink
rule.rule_desc: Detects command execution APIs requiring strict input control review.
rule.vuln_desc: User-influenced command execution can lead to OS command injection.
rule.developer: Avoid shell invocation or enforce strict allow-lists and escaping.
rule.reviewer: Trace untrusted data flow into command execution calls. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Freertos Insecure Transport/Cert Bypass Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects TLS verification disablement in C HTTP clients.
rule.vuln_desc: Disabling TLS verification allows MITM attacks.
rule.developer: Enable strict certificate and hostname verification.
rule.reviewer: Audit transport settings in network code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Freertos Hardcoded Secret Material
rule.rule_desc: Detects likely hardcoded secret values in C source.
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded credentials can be extracted from firmware/binaries.
rule.reviewer: Review credential handling strategy and key rotation. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Freertos FreeRTOS Privileged API Review Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects privileged task/queue operations that require security review context.
rule.vuln_desc: Improper task privilege and IPC handling can create reliability and security risks.
rule.developer: Apply least privilege and validate message boundary checks.
rule.reviewer: Review task privilege and queue data validation boundaries. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
